scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B) events — Both PRs alter the events table (Alan adds webhook_status and delivery_attempts; Sarah adds delivery_state and processor_version).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text:
C

Sarah will be unavailable on Thursday March 13 because she only works Monday, Wednesday, and Friday, and she’s explicitly off that day.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text:
B

SAML SSO is not covered. It was explicitly scoped out of the auth migration phase (no SAML in this release) even though it was promised to Acme after the migration.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new onboarding flow shrank from 8 steps to 5 and removed four steps (including the first milestone activation trigger), so the definition of “completion” has changed. The 60% vs 85% aren’t apples-to-apples.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text:
C

Staging-3 was created for load testing but never actually used for load testing (last deploy: Jan 22).

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — All three tickets show "Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25," indicating they were in the same migration batch.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: The design doc summary lists two open questions: 1) Cursor vs offset pagination, and 2) Webhook payload format (nested vs flat). All other items have implicit consensus.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B) src/billing/mapping.rs — Alan calls it 'the rosetta stone file' and it maps between plan_id, tier_id, and subscription naming across the codebase.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text:
B

CSV only. Josh’s research shows all three prospects are satisfied with CSV (with the right columns); no customer requested PDF, and the Excel request came from the sales rep, not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: The test distribution shows 0 edge case tests and 7 end-to-end tests (End-to-end 7, Edge case 0). It also lists 280 unit tests (82%) and 55 integration tests (16%), totaling 342.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: In the thread, Sarah proposed a synthesis: JWT tokens that feel like API keys with long-lived tokens and bearer auth, combining JWT security (rotation, revocation) with API-key simplicity for developers.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: The webhook section notes “No delivery confirmation mechanism — if a webhook is sent and the customer's endpoint returns 200 but doesn't actually process it, we have no way to know.” This creates silent failures that could go unnoticed until reconciliation (invoices don’t match).

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text:
B

Takeshi at NovaTech should weigh in—the decision context notes no customer perspective has been consulted yet, and Takeshi offered to review from an API consumer perspective.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text:
C

About 2.75 hours per week (1h hardcoded URLs + 45m retry logic + 1h error messages + missed bugs from skipped test), per the combined calculation in the tech debt memo.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text:
C

Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked about Ed25519. It’s cheap to add because Sarah’s webhook code already has a signature module, so the change is localized to that module (roughly half a day).

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text:
C

Explanation: The workspace projection states “Break-even at feature 5; net positive from feature 6 onward,” so the refactor pays for itself at feature 5 (with profits starting from feature 6).

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

Explanation: The root cause is ambiguity in the spec—“subscription start date” could mean Jan 15 (signup) or Feb 1 (billing period). Engineering implemented Feb 1, but it’s a spec interpretation issue, not a backend bug or data issue. A 1-line UI change (or show both dates) would fix it.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Annual billing would require proration, refund, and dunning changes that would conflict with the March 31 billing migration launch (risking timing and the reconciliation deadline). This aligns with the project plan’s tight March timeline and blockers.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text:
B

- Monitoring detected the issue in 12 minutes (alert at 09:12).
- Root cause classified as a process gap: insufficient testing against production-scale data with full historical records (legacy subscriptions missing the plan_change_date field).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — 95% utilization at baseline; during typical spikes (1.5–2x) the combined 143–190 req/sec exceeds the 100 req/sec limit, causing 429 errors.
